run_summary: Portfolio is +0.56% on mark-to-market with 4 open positions showing mixed signals. NTLA and PTCT remain intact on their 8-K/catalyst theses despite modest unrealized gains; ALGN is slightly underwater on consumer discretionary positioning but thesis is still live; ALK thesis (Iran deal → oil lower → fuel relief) remains structurally sound. New flag pool is dominated by post-earnings positioning and sector sympathy plays—most are SKIP or WATCH, with two high-conviction WATCH candidates (AVAV and AYI re-entries that fail the re-entry bar despite new earnings data) and one legitimate exploratory BUY (AEIS sector sympathy fade). No new conviction-tier entries justified.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Apparent sympathy with SMH -3.97% sector dump (AVGO -3.67%, ORCL -2.58%, ONTO -5.9%, VECO -5.96%); no AEIS-specific 8-K or news.
entries.thesis: AEIS has no 8-K, no earnings imminent, and is classified as Industrial/Electrical Equipment yet derives substantial revenue from semiconductor capital equipment customers. SMH is down nearly 4% and ONTO/VECO are down 6% — AEIS is getting tarred with the same brush despite its more diversified end-market mix (also industrial, data center, medical). On 4.9x average volume with no news, this looks like a sympathy washout that retraces faster than the pure-play semicap names once the SMH downdraft stabilizes.
entries.what_kills: A late-breaking 8-K with a customer-loss disclosure or guide-down, sector weakness extending on confirmed cap-ex cuts from a major fab customer, or AEIS-specific negative sell-side note.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (4 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 on sympathy washout with semiconductor capital equipment names (SMH -3.97%, ONTO -5.9%, VECO -5.96%). AEIS is -7.21% on 4.88x volume with no company-specific catalyst. Flag thesis: AEIS has diversified end-markets (industrial, data center, medical, not just semicap) and historically over-correlates to SMH on risk-off days and then decouples faster on recovery. This is a named, mechanical setup with a clear confirm signal (SMH stabilizes, AEIS bounces harder than pure semicap plays) and a clear kill signal (AEIS-specific 8-K with customer loss or guide cut). Confidence is 3; exploratory 6% is appropriately sized. The thesis is coherent and testable: we are long the view that sector-sympathy sells in diversified suppliers decouple on recovery faster than pure-plays. Volume/price setup supports the hypothesis (heavy capitulation on imminent good news). Exploratory tier justified—this is not a conviction move, but it carries named mechanism and clear signal structure.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes SMH will stabilize imminently and that AEIS will decouple faster on recovery, but provides no catalyst or timeframe anchor for when this stabilization occurs.
red_team.critique: The thesis rests on a mechanical 'sympathy washout' that is self-referential: AEIS has fallen 7.21% in 5 days on no company-specific news, so the argument is that it will bounce faster than pure-plays when the sector recovers. However, the bull case does not specify what triggers SMH stabilization or how long the portfolio is willing to wait. Semiconductor capital equipment as a sector could extend weakness on macro chip-cycle concerns, earnings revisions, or China/Taiwan trade escalation—none of which Haiku addresses as a kill mechanism beyond the stated 'AEIS-specific 8-K' or 'confirmed cap-ex cuts from a major fab customer.' If SMH continues to decline or trades sideways for weeks, AEIS could grind lower in sympathy regardless of its diversified end-markets, making the 'faster decouple' thesis untestable on the stated days timeframe. The exploratory 6% sizing offers some hedge against this timing risk, but the core mechanism—that sympathy sells decouple faster—is asserted without a historical win rate or a named recovery trigger.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q4 earnings 8-K filed 2026-06-29 (items 2.02, 7.01, 9.01) following a 2026-06-25 8-K with 5.02 officer change item.
entries.thesis: Sharp move is anchored by a same-day 2.02 earnings 8-K and a recent 5.02 officer change, against a backdrop of defense-budget tailwinds and risk-on tape. Volume ran ~4x average, suggesting institutions are repricing on the print rather than retail chasing. With no obvious overreaction signal (no negative items like 2.05/2.06/4.02 in the filings) and ITA also up, the magnitude looks broadly justified for a small-cap defense name beating into a budget-cycle narrative.
entries.what_kills: If the 5.02 turns out to be a CFO/COO unplanned departure, or if the beat was driven by one-time items (e.g., tax benefit) with guide flat/down — then the move is OVERDONE and should fade.
entries.reasoning: RE-ENTRY WARNING ACTIVE: You exited AVAV 5 days ago for a -4.75% loss on UNDERDONE conf 4 thesis (4.02 restatement + 5.02 officer departure + earnings risk). New flag is RATIONAL conf 3 on today's +18.76% earnings reaction. To re-enter, the re-entry bar requires: (1) confidence >= 4 (flag is conf 3—FAILS); (2) genuinely new information not present at exit. The new information IS the earnings print itself (which did not exist 5 days ago)—that IS new and material. However, confidence is only 3, and the execution layer will downgrade any BUY to WATCH. The flag's logic is solid (defense budget tailwind, clean 2.02, no negative items, 4.3x volume suggests institutional repricing)—but the re-entry bar on a recently-closed loss is confidence >= 4. This is confidence 3. Decision: WATCH, not BUY. If the stock holds gains tomorrow and sell-side upgrades emerge (pushing conviction to 4+), re-evaluate.

entries.name: Mercury Systems Inc
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst detected; likely sympathy with AVAV earnings and broader defense/ITA strength.
entries.thesis: MRCY is up double-digits with volume_multiple of only 1.07 and no 8-K, no scheduled earnings — meaning institutions are not heavily participating. The move looks like sympathy with AVAV's print plus the defense-budget narrative. Without conviction-level volume, double-digit sympathy moves in defense names tend to give back a meaningful portion within days as the news-specific tailwind to AVAV doesn't translate cleanly to MRCY's different business mix (embedded computing vs. drones).
entries.what_kills: Disclosure of an actual MRCY-specific catalyst (contract win, M&A rumor) emerges; or volume picks up materially on follow-through day signaling real institutional accumulation.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 on sympathy with AVAV earnings. Thesis is clean: +10.99% on 1.07x volume with no 8-K and no scheduled catalyst. Sympathy moves in defense peers without institutional participation (volume_multiple ~1x) are unreliable and tend to fade within days. Confidence in the fade is high, but the setup is inherently a SHORT, not a BUY. Portfolio has no short mandate and existing defense positioning (AVAV-adjacent thesis) is already covered by AVAV WATCH. Skip.

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K with annual-meeting-pattern items (5.02 director matters, 5.07 shareholder vote, 9.01 exhibits); broader XBI/XLV strength.
entries.thesis: 8-K items 5.02 + 5.07 + 9.01 is the classic annual meeting / director election / proxy-vote-results combination — administrative, not material. The barely-positive move on heavy volume against a strongly green XBI/XLV tape suggests the stock is mostly riding the sector rather than reacting to anything filing-specific. Magnitude appears appropriate to the catalyst (which is essentially nil at the company level).
entries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be an unexpected senior officer departure rather than routine board rotation, which would reframe the day's relative underperformance vs XBI as bearish.
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 on routine governance 8-K (5.02 director, 5.07 vote, 9.01 exhibits). The thesis is explicitly that the filing is administrative and immaterial; the stock is riding sector (XBI/XLV up). No alpha signal here—this is noise. The move is +0.79%, entirely explained by sector beta. Skip.

entries.name: Acuity Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q4 earnings release filed via 8-K item 2.02
entries.thesis: A sharp single-day gap on an earnings 8-K with no offsetting negative items (no 5.02, no 4.02, no impairment) suggests a clean beat-and-raise. Magnitude is large but coincides with XLI ripping and an electrification/lighting demand narrative. Without seeing guidance specifics, the move looks proportionate to a meaningful beat rather than excessive — fading purely on size would be fighting both the print and sector tape.
entries.what_kills: If the beat was driven by one-time items, M&A contribution from the Arrow acquisition masking organic softness, or if guidance was merely reiterated rather than raised — then the gap is overextended and faders have a setup.
entries.reasoning: RE-ENTRY WARNING ACTIVE: You exited AYI 13 days ago for a realized +2.77% gain on OVERDONE conf 3 thesis (sympathy fade on CPI/Iran headlines). New flag is RATIONAL conf 3 on today's +17.64% earnings reaction (2.02 item, clean beat, XLI supportive, no negative items). To re-enter after a win: re-entry bar is (1) confidence >= 4 and (2) genuinely new information. New information IS present: the earnings print itself (which did not exist at exit)—and the flag's thesis is distinct from the prior sympathy-fade thesis. This is a clean beat with no offsetting bad news, contrasting with the prior flag's weakness thesis. HOWEVER: confidence is only conf 3, and the execution layer will downgrade BUY to WATCH. The flag's logic is sound (beat + raise + XLI green + no negatives = gap likely sustainable), but confidence must reach 4 to clear the re-entry bar on a recently-closed win. Decision: WATCH. If sell-side revisions emerge tomorrow and press release confirms organic acceleration + FY guide raise, revisit with elevated conviction.

entries.name: Calix, Inc
entries.catalyst: Unknown — elevated volume without visible news
entries.thesis: CALX -3% today on 2.66x average volume — the volume spike is the interesting signal, not the price move. No 8-K, no scheduled earnings, no obvious peer drag (broadband-access equipment isn't part of the SMH wreckage). High-volume selling in a $2.3B name without identifiable catalyst often reflects fund-level repositioning that reverses when the seller is done. Tech-sector beta weak today provides cover but doesn't fully explain. Modest fade setup if next session shows volume normalizing.
entries.what_kills: BEAD/rural broadband funding cut announcement or major tier-1 telco capex revision — this would justify and extend the move
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 on small-cap selling on elevated volume with no catalyst. Flag thesis is that 2.66x volume on -3% without visible news suggests fund-level unwind that reverses when seller is done. Logic is plausible but does not meet BUY bar: (1) no named catalyst URL; (2) thesis is entirely speculative ("unknown seller repositioning") rather than grounded in analyst signal or filing. Watching volume and waiting for either a bounce + volume normalization (confirming the fade thesis) or negative news (BEAD cuts, capex revision) is the right move. Exploratory tier would be a stretch here because the setup lacks concrete catalyst or triggering mechanism. WATCH.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sector beta — semi rally and advanced packaging narrative; no company-specific signal in 8-K or earnings calendar.
entries.thesis: ONTO up ~8% on the day and ~20% over five days, on a tape where SMH is +3.78% and TSM is +4.94%. Volume_multiple 1.73x suggests real participation. With no 8-K and no scheduled earnings, this looks like a continuation of the chip-rally beta trade tied to advanced packaging exposure — the move is roughly proportional to the sector lift plus a momentum premium. Not an obvious mispricing in either direction.
entries.what_kills: Sharp reversal in SMH leads to outsized ONTO drawdown — would suggest beta-leverage was the entire move and the name is over-owned.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: None identified; below-average volume and no 8-K or earnings within window.
entries.thesis: VECO gapped ~9% but volume_multiple is only 0.86x — meaning the move happened on light tape. Five-day change only +1.3%, so this is a single-day pop not part of a trend. Sector tailwind (SMH +3.78%) supports some of the lift but doesn't explain the outsized beta. Without an identifiable catalyst and without volume confirmation, I cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: Volume stays muted and the move retraces within 1-2 sessions — confirms it was thin-tape noise.

entries.name: Badger Meter, Inc.
entries.thesis: BMI isn't a semi or defense name, so the sector-rotation narrative doesn't apply cleanly — it's a scientific instruments / water utilities adjacent business. Volume is below average (0.84x) yet five-day move is +16%, suggesting persistent accumulation without a single news catalyst. Could be a sell-side note, a peer-derived read-through (Xylem, Roper), or M&A speculation — but with no concrete evidence, neither bull nor bear case clears the conf-3 bar.
entries.what_kills: If the 5-day move is purely momentum/short-covering with no fundamental catalyst, a quick fade is plausible — but I cannot assert that directionally without more data.

entries.name: Repligen Corporation

entries.thesis: RGEN -6.8% on 1.57x volume with XBI +1.9% is a meaningful divergence. No 8-K, no scheduled earnings. The Regeneron FDA PreCheck headline is for REGN (different company) — possible ticker confusion unwind if traders bought RGEN by mistake earlier and are now reversing, but I cannot confirm that mechanism from available data. Without a clear catalyst I can't articulate either side at conf 3+.

entries.name: Five Below, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possible continuation of tariff/import-cost concern, but no specific catalyst in feed
entries.thesis: FIVE -5.4% on average volume with XLY +2.4% is a sharp relative-strength break (~8% underperformance). Five-day is -7.9%, so this is a continuation of weakness, not a one-day surprise. No 8-K, no earnings imminent. Possible tariff/import-cost narrative since FIVE is heavily China-sourced and a risk-on tape might be lifting names with better domestic exposure while leaving import-dependent names behind — but I can't anchor that at conf 3+ without a specific headline.
entries.what_kills: If DLTR/DG/OLLI are up with sector, FIVE-specific issue (channel check, mgmt commentary) is implied; if they're all down, it's a discount-retail narrative.

entries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no detectable catalyst in feed
entries.thesis: JBLU -6.2% is a notable divergence on a risk-on day with oil weakening (which should help airlines) and XLI +0.86%. Volume below average (0.9x), suggesting this isn't capitulation but possibly a forced seller or a single broker action. No 8-K, no earnings near. JBLU is a low-priced, high-short-interest, balance-sheet-stressed name where moves of this size are routine and often technical. Can't articulate either direction at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: If peer airlines (LUV, AAL, UAL, ALK) also down 3-5%, sector narrative; if JBLU alone, idiosyncratic (Spirit-related credit, route news, etc.).

entries.name: National Beverage Corp.

Could be pre-earnings de-risking on weak channel checks (LaCroix shelf data) or could be illiquid retail flow. Cannot articulate either direction at conf 3+ without checking syndicated scanner data.

entries.name: Manhattan Associates

entries.thesis: MANH popping strongly while XLK is down nearly 2% is highly unusual for a quality SaaS name — the relative move is the signal, but with no 8-K, no earnings, and no public news identified, I cannot determine whether this is a takeout rumor, analyst upgrade, customer win, or other. The magnitude warrants attention but I cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the catalyst.
entries.what_kills: Move reverses on Monday with no news identified, suggesting it was a technical or quant-flow artifact.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security

Today's bounce on heavy volume could be a dead-cat bounce or the start of a relief rally. With AVAV (peer drone/defense name) just having filed a 4.02 restatement, there's a possibility of contagion fear in the unmanned-systems subspace that may or may not be justified for KTOS specifically.

entries.name: A.O. Smith Corporation
entries.catalyst: 8-K dated 2026-06-22 disclosing officer/director departure or appointment
entries.thesis: A 5.02 officer change can cut either direction — planned retirement vs forced exit vs new strategic hire. The stock is rallying meaningfully on a day when industrials broadly are strong (XLI +1.2%), so part of the move is sector beta. Without reading the 8-K exhibit to determine whether this is a CEO succession, an activist-driven replacement, or a routine board change, the directional read isn't articulable at conf 3+.

entries.thesis: BLDR's double-digit move aligns with the Trump housing-bill / Save America Act news cycle (housing affordability legislation discussion, possible interest rate pressure narrative) and broad industrial/small-cap risk-on (XLI +1.2%, IWM +0.5%). No 8-K, no earnings catalyst — this looks like macro positioning into a housing-policy event combined with sector rotation. Magnitude is large but matches the macro setup; no clear over/underreaction edge.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: 6/18 Reg FD 8-K of unclear content; ongoing met-coal commodity weakness
entries.thesis: AMR down nearly 5% today and ~12% over 5 days, with a 7.01 Reg FD disclosure filed last week. Reg FD items are catch-all — could be investor presentation, could be guidance update — and without the filing contents we cannot tell which. Coking coal pricing is also pressured by global steel demand uncertainty. Cannot construct a 3+ confidence call in either direction without reading the 6/18 8-K exhibit.
entries.what_kills: Filing turns out to be benign (conference presentation) — then today's selling is pure commodity tape and likely RATIONAL given met-coal spot weakness
